mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate compresses the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and signals accommodative monetary policy, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.5% do not produce positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above nine percent indicates severe labor market slack that suppresses aggregate wage income and consumer spending expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in retail sales and housing starts captures a broad household sector deleveraging that reduces corporate revenue forecasts; the interaction of RSAFS and HOUST predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days beyond either series alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST does not significantly improve prediction of 5-day returns (p > 0.05) versus a single-series model over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero lower bound regime, accelerations in consumer prices signal successful reflation and impending nominal growth recovery, which lifts equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: During months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%, month-over-month CPIAUCSL acceleration fails to produce positive 20-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.02 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contractions in industrial production reduce expected corporate earnings and signal underutilized manufacturing capacity, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Negative month-over-month changes in INDPRO are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below twenty-five basis points triggers expectations of extended monetary backstop and unconventional policy support, which generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of declining payroll employment and an elevated unemployment rate confirms a contracting labor market that depresses consumer confidence and spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signals of falling PAYEMS and UNRATE above 8% fail to predict negative 5-day returns in more than 60% of concurrent observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly contractions in real GDP reduce expected aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days as recessionary earnings revisions materialize.
failure_condition: Quarter-over-quarter declines in GDPC1 are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 10-year rolling window.
